title: CLAUDE.md
text: mSigPlot R package plotting mutational signatures mutational spectra including SBS (single base substitution), DBS (doublet base substitution), indel catalogs. creates publication-quality ggplot2 bar plots (heatmaps DBS136) 10 catalog types.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: plot_guess(catalog, ...) plot_guess_pdf(catalog, filename, ...) auto-detect catalog type row count dispatch appropriate function. catalog type paired plot_*() (single sample, returns ggplot) plot_*_pdf() (multi-sample PDF, 5 per page via gridExtra::grid.arrange + cairo_pdf).

title: CLAUDE.md
text: catalog_row_order() returns named list canonical row name vectors supported types (SBS96, SBS192, SBS1536, DBS78, DBS136, DBS144, ID, ID166, ID89, ID476). Strand-aware types (SBS192, DBS144) internal reorder_*_for_plotting() helpers interleave transcribed/untranscribed pairs. DBS136 uses order_DBS136_for_plotting() map 136 rows 160-position heatmap grid.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: 476-channel indel type table generated type_476_indel_type() (R/type_476_indel_type.R). 89-channel table defined inline within plot_89(). produce data frames columns: IndelType, Indel, Indel3, Figlabel.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Y-axis auto-detection: sum(catalog) < 1.1, label “Proportion”; otherwise “Count”. functions also support catalog.type attribute catalog object ("counts", "counts.signature", "density", "density.signature"). show_counts parameter: NULL (auto based catalog type), TRUE (force), FALSE (suppress). Controls per-class count labels. Text size parameters: Named *_cex (multipliers relative base_size). Deprecated *_size aliases still accepted warnings; specifying old new errors. Color palettes: Hardcoded per function consistent publication figures. PDF export: *_pdf() functions use cairo_pdf() arrange 5 plots per page (except DBS136 heatmaps: 1 per page). plot_complex parameter: Indel plots (89, 476) optional Complex channel, default.

title: Plotting mutational signatures with mSigPlot
text: mSigPlot creates publication-quality plots mutational signatures mutational spectra. supports SBS (single base substitution), DBS (doublet base substitution), indel catalogs across 10 classification systems.
code: library(mSigPlot)

title: Plotting mutational signatures with mSigPlot
text: mSigPlot works numeric vectors, data frames, matrices, tibbles, data.tables. load three example catalogs bundled package.

title: Plotting mutational signatures with mSigPlot
text: 96-channel SBS catalog one row per trinucleotide mutation context. bundled example contains mutation counts four HepG2 samples.
code: sbs96_file <- system.file("extdata", "sbs96_example.csv", package = "mSigPlot") sbs96_df <- read.csv(sbs96_file)  # Build a named catalog: row names are the 96 canonical trinucleotide types orders <- catalog_row_order() catalog_sbs96 <- data.frame(   sample1 = sbs96_df[, 3],   row.names = orders$SBS96 )  head(catalog_sbs96) #>      sample1 #> ACAA     123 #> ACCA     390 #> ACGA      15 #> ACTA     194 #> CCAA     212 #> CCCA     113

title: Plotting mutational signatures with mSigPlot
text: 78-channel DBS catalog covers doublet base substitution classes.
code: dbs78_file <- system.file("extdata", "dbs78_example.csv", package = "mSigPlot") dbs78_df <- read.csv(dbs78_file)  catalog_dbs78 <- data.frame(   sample1 = dbs78_df[, 3],   row.names = paste0(dbs78_df$Ref, dbs78_df$Var) )  head(catalog_dbs78) #>      sample1 #> ACCA       0 #> ACCG       0 #> ACCT       0 #> ACGA       0 #> ACGG       0 #> ACGT       0

title: Plotting mutational signatures with mSigPlot
text: 83-channel indel catalog uses COSMIC classification. file contains COSMIC v3.5 reference signatures; column signature.
code: id83_file <- system.file("extdata", "id83_cosmic_v3.5.tsv", package = "mSigPlot") id83_sigs <- read.table(id83_file, header = TRUE, sep = "\t",                          row.names = 1, check.names = FALSE)  # Pick one signature to plot catalog_id83 <- id83_sigs[, "ID1", drop = FALSE] head(catalog_id83) #>                     ID1 #> DEL:C:1:0  1.598890e-04 #> DEL:C:1:1  7.735230e-04 #> DEL:C:1:2  3.310000e-18 #> DEL:C:1:3  1.907613e-03 #> DEL:C:1:4  7.059900e-04 #> DEL:C:1:5+ 3.370115e-03

title: Plotting mutational signatures with mSigPlot
code: plot_SBS96(catalog_sbs96, plot_title = "HepG2 sample — SBS96")

title: Plotting mutational signatures with mSigPlot
code: plot_DBS78(catalog_dbs78, plot_title = "HepG2 sample — DBS78")

title: Plotting mutational signatures with mSigPlot
code: plot_83(catalog_id83, plot_title = "COSMIC ID1 signature")

title: Plotting mutational signatures with mSigPlot
text: plot functions accept parameters control appearance:
code: plot_SBS96(   catalog_sbs96,   plot_title = "Custom styling example",   base_size   = 14,   show_counts = TRUE,   grid        = FALSE )

title: Plotting mutational signatures with mSigPlot
text: don’t know (don’t want specify) catalog type, plot_guess() detects number rows:
code: plot_guess(catalog_sbs96, plot_title = "Auto-detected SBS96")

title: Plotting mutational signatures with mSigPlot
text: batch plotting, every plot function _pdf() variant writes multi-page PDF 5 plots per page. auto-dispatch version plot_guess_pdf():
code: # Build a multi-sample matrix (4 samples) sbs96_mat <- as.matrix(sbs96_df[, 3:6]) rownames(sbs96_mat) <- orders$SBS96 colnames(sbs96_mat) <- paste0("Sample_", 1:4)  # Write to PDF — 5 plots per page plot_guess_pdf(sbs96_mat, file.path(tempdir(), "sbs96_samples.pdf"))

title: Plotting mutational signatures with mSigPlot
text: Since plot functions return ggplot objects, can combine using packages like patchwork:
code: if (requireNamespace("patchwork", quietly = TRUE)) {   library(patchwork)   p1 <- plot_SBS96(catalog_sbs96, plot_title = "SBS96")   p2 <- plot_DBS78(catalog_dbs78, plot_title = "DBS78")   p1 / p2 }

title: Mutational signature plotting functions
text: Publication-quality plots mutational signatures mutational spectra. Supports SBS, DBS, indel catalogs across 10 classification systems bar charts, strand-bias plots, heatmaps.

title: Mutational signature plotting functions
code: # Install from GitHub install.packages("remotes")  # if needed remotes::install_github("steverozen/mSigPlot")

title: Mutational signature plotting functions
text: Every plot function accepts numeric vector, single-column data.frame, matrix, tibble, data.table. easiest entry point plot_guess(), auto-detects catalog type row count:
code: library(mSigPlot)  # Auto-detect and plot plot_guess(my_catalog)  # Or call a specific function plot_SBS96(sbs96_catalog, plot_title = "Sample 1")  # Export multiple samples to a multi-page PDF (5 per page) plot_guess_pdf(multi_sample_catalog, "output.pdf")

title: Mutational signature plotting functions
text: Every plot function except plot_SBS288 corresponding _pdf() variant (e.g., plot_SBS96_pdf()) writes multi-sample PDFs 5 plots per page.

title: Mutational signature plotting functions
text: 96-channel single base substitution profile 6 color-coded mutation classes (C>, C>G, C>T, T>, T>C, T>G).

title: Mutational signature plotting functions
text: Transcription strand-aware SBS profile. Bars paired: transcribed (blue) untranscribed (red) trinucleotide context. Intergenic regions plotted.

title: Mutational signature plotting functions
text: Pentanucleotide-context SBS shown six 16x16 heatmaps.

title: Mutational signature plotting functions
text: 288-channel SBS profile split transcription strand: template (transcribed), non-template (untranscribed), -transcribed (intergenic). Displayed three stacked SBS96 panels shared y-axis.

title: Mutational signature plotting functions
text: 78-channel DBS (doublet base substitution) profile across 10 dinucleotide reference classes.

title: Mutational signature plotting functions
text: DBS shown 10 small 4x4 heatmaps, one per dinucleotide class, maxima-per-class summary.

title: Mutational signature plotting functions
text: COSMIC 83-type indel classification covering 1bp deletions/insertions, repeat-mediated indels, microhomology-mediated deletions.

text: 89-type indel classification system.

title: Mutational signature plotting functions
text: plot functions share parameters: Bar chart functions also accept grid, upper, xlabels, ylabels toggles. See ?plot_SBS96 full parameter list.

title: Mutational signature plotting functions
text: catalog row names matching canonical mutation type labels, mSigPlot validates reorders automatically. row names absent (unnamed vector sequential integer names), data assumed canonical order. Use catalog_row_order() inspect expected order catalog type:
code: head(catalog_row_order()$SBS96) #> [1] "ACAA" "ACCA" "ACGA" "ACTA" "CCAA" "CCCA"

title: Return catalog row orders for all supported catalog types — catalog_row_order
text: Returns named list containing canonical row ordering catalog type. used validation ordering mutation catalogs.

code: catalog_row_order()

title: Return catalog row orders for all supported catalog types — catalog_row_order
text: named list elements: SBS96, SBS192, SBS1536, DBS78, DBS136, DBS144, ID (83-category COSMIC indels), ID166, ID89, ID476.

what: Normalize catalog input to a 1-column data.frame — normalize_catalog
title: Normalize catalog input to a 1-column data.frame — normalize_catalog
text: Internal helper coerces catalog supported input type (numeric vector, matrix, data.table, tibble, data.frame) 1-column data.frame validated row names.

title: Normalize catalog input to a 1-column data.frame — normalize_catalog
code: normalize_catalog(   catalog,   expected_nrow,   canonical_order = NULL,   type_label = "" )

title: Normalize catalog input to a 1-column data.frame — normalize_catalog
text: catalog Input catalog supported format. expected_nrow Integer. Expected number rows/elements. canonical_order Character vector canonical row names, NULL skip row name validation. type_label Character label error/warning messages (e.g. "SBS96").

title: Normalize catalog input to a 1-column data.frame — normalize_catalog
text: 1-column data.frame, NULL row names match canonical order.

title: Return order for DBS136 plotting — order_DBS136_for_plotting
text: Returns 160-element character vector mapping 136-row DBS catalog 160-position layout (10 groups 16 10 heatmap images). 24 entries DBS136 catalog serve NA placeholders.

code: order_DBS136_for_plotting()

title: Return order for DBS136 plotting — order_DBS136_for_plotting
text: 160-element character vector row names DBS136 heatmap layout.

title: Plot 476-channel indel profile — plot_476
